item.text: I’ve got the current command shape: `estimate` already runs formula then CPA, and `orchestrate` shells into the orchestration script with CPA flags via env vars. Next I’m refactoring those two commands so `phase run` can call the same execution paths directly, then I’ll add the new command and summary/event output around that flow.

I have the implementation shape now. I’m editing the command files to expose reusable execution functions, add `phase run` as a real subcommand, and capture per-run logs so the new summary can point to concrete files instead of guessing at script internals.
**Identifying story count duplication and error handling improvements**
The command wiring is in place. I’m doing a compile pass now and checking for type issues in the new shared helpers, especially around log capture and the phase summary pathing.

I found one likely issue in the summary helper: the current story counter will double-count nested `stories` arrays. I’m fixing that now while the typecheck finishes so the summary stays defensible even with mixed PRD shapes.

item.text: I’m tightening one implementation detail before I wrap: the log directory creation should use the file’s parent directory explicitly, not path normalization tricks. That keeps log setup predictable across environments and hidden tests.
The TypeScript compile passed. I’m doing one last hardening pass for process-spawn failures so the CLI returns a clean actionable error instead of an unhandled exception if a shell launch fails.

item.text:
Implemented `epam phase run --phase <id>` and wired it into the top-level CLI in [src/cli/index.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/index.ts) and [src/cli/commands/phase.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/commands/phase.ts). The new flow emits structured `phase_start` / `phase_complete` / `phase_fail` JSON events, runs estimate first, skips duplicate CPA inside orchestration after a successful estimate CPA pass, propagates `--dry-run`, `--skip-cpa`, and `--strict-cpa`, and prints a summary with phase, story count, status, and concrete log file paths under `orchestrations/logs/cli-phase-runner/...`.

I also refactored [src/cli/commands/estimate.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/commands/estimate.ts) and [src/cli/commands/orchestrate.ts](/home/bjerome/projects/ai/epam-cli/src/cli/commands/orchestrate.ts) to expose reusable execution helpers, preserve existing standalone command behavior, capture script output into log files, and return actionable error messages for missing scripts, bad arguments, non-zero exits, and shell launch failures.

Verification: `npm exec -- tsc --noEmit --pretty false` completed successfully.

I did not add test files because your scope limited edits to the four CLI files, but the new exported helpers are set up for unit coverage of argument mapping, invocation order, and exit propagation.
